<!--
Thanks for contributing to Trustfall Lite. Please fill out this
template so the reviewer has the context they need.

Trustfall Lite is a security tool. Changes are reviewed adversarially.
Be specific about what you changed and why.
-->

## Summary

<!-- One or two sentences. What does this PR do? -->

## Related issue

<!-- Closes #123, or "no issue" if this is a small fix or doc patch. -->

## Changes

<!--
Bullet list of what changed. Include any:
- New dependencies (justify each one)
- Breaking changes (call out explicitly)
- Behavior changes that aren't covered by existing tests
-->

## Testing

<!--
- [ ] All existing tests pass locally (`pytest`)
- [ ] New code has tests that cover the new behavior
- [ ] If this changes user-visible CLI behavior, README/INSTALL/DIFF_SPEC have been updated
- [ ] If this adds new machine-readable identifiers (status values, JSON keys, exit codes), they are documented in the locked spec docs
-->

## Checklist

- [ ] I have read `CONTRIBUTING.md`
- [ ] I have signed my commits (DCO)
- [ ] No new third-party dependencies, or new deps are justified above
- [ ] No secrets, tokens, or private artifacts are included in the diff
- [ ] If this is a security fix, I have NOT described the vulnerability publicly here (see SECURITY.md)
